channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Pakistan Conducts Airstrikes on Terrorist Camps in Afghanistan
item.description: On the night of 12th to 13th March 2026, Pakistan carried out targeted airstrikes inside Afghanistan against terrorist installations, according to Information Minister Attaullah Tarar. The strikes foc...

item.title: Families of 176 Woro Residents Abducted by Terrorists Express Fear for Vulnerable Captives
item.description: Families of 176 people abducted from Woro community in Kaiama Local Government Area, Kwara State, dey fear for the lives of pregnant women and children among dem because both the terrorists and govern...

item.title: Tanzania Promotes Tourism to Northern European Markets Through Oslo Roadshow
item.description: Tanzania don intensify efforts to attract tourists from Northern Europe as the My Tanzania Roadshow 2026 continue for Oslo. The event bring together both Tanzanian and Norwegian tourism stakeholders t...

item.title: UK Economy Shows Minimal Growth as Middle East Conflict Drives Energy Inflation
item.description: The UK economy grew by a modest 0.2% in the three months leading to January, with no growth recorded in January itself, according to official figures from the Office for National Statistics (ONS). Ind...

item.title: Israel Conducts Additional Airstrikes on Iranian Military Sites
item.description: Israel announced fresh airstrikes targeting military sites throughout Iran amid the ongoing US-Israel-Iran conflict that erupted in late February 2026. The Israeli Defense Forces (IDF) reported striki...

item.title: Numbeo Ranks Five South African Cities Among the World’s Most Unsafe Urban Areas
item.description: A new report from global data platform Numbeo has revealed that Pretoria and Johannesburg are among the world's most dangerous cities in 2026, with Pretoria ranked second and Johannesburg fifth in the...

item.title: Polio cases decline in Pakistan despite security concerns along Pak-Afghan border
item.description: Data from Pakistan’s Polio Eradication Programme shows that during the first two months of 2026, most environmental samples for poliovirus have tested negative, indicating a significant improvement co...

item.title: ACMobility Reports Significant Growth in EV Sales and Market Share in FY2025
item.description: ACMobility, the automotive division of the Ayala Group, has reported a remarkable increase in its total unit sales for the fiscal year 2025, reaching 42,684 units, almost double compared to last year’...

item.title: Raiders struggle to regain momentum in heavy defeat against Warriors
item.description: The Canberra Raiders faced a harsh night in Auckland, falling 40-6 to the New Zealand Warriors in a match that highlighted their struggles to maintain momentum. Raiders captain Joe Tapine emphasised t...

item.title: Ndindi Nyoro Clarifies False Arrest Rumours, Confirms Constituency Activities
item.description: Kiharu MP Ndindi Nyoro has come out clearly to dismiss rumours suggesting he was arrested. The viral video circulating online, which many thought showed him in police custody, is actually from 2019 an...

item.title: Whistler Blackcomb closes section of ski resort following early-morning rockslide
item.description: Early on Thursday morning, before the Whistler Blackcomb ski resort opened, a rockslide occurred on Whistler Peak. Fortunately, no one was in the area at the time, and no injuries were reported. The s...

item.title: Seven-year-old boy tragically killed in road collision in Sutton-in-Ashfield
item.description: A tragic incident occurred on Tuesday, 10 March, in Sutton-in-Ashfield, Nottinghamshire, when seven-year-old Logan Parker was fatally struck by a car on the A38 Kings Mill Road East near Kingfisher Wa...

item.title: Sensex and Nifty fall due to West Asia tensions, higher oil prices and foreign fund outflows
item.description: On 13th March 2026, Indian stock markets faced significant losses as the Sensex and Nifty ended sharply lower, continuing a three-day decline. The main triggers were rising geopolitical tensions in We...

item.title: Rotimi Salami talks about raising a child even if DNA proves he’s not the father
item.description: Nollywood actor Rotimi Salami don open mouth on one matter wey dey touch paternity and how e dey handle family. For interview wey e do with BBC Yoruba, Salami yan say even if DNA test show say pikin n...

item.title: Greg James undertakes 1000km charity cycling challenge following father's stroke
item.description: Greg James, the 40-year-old Radio 1 DJ, has commenced an eight-day, 1000km tandem cycling challenge for Comic Relief, just a week after his father suffered a stroke during heart surgery, leaving his c...

item.title: Warriors' Young Squad Earns Praise from Slater After Canberra Win
item.description: The Warriors' young players have caught the eye of former NRL star Billy Slater following their commanding performance against the Canberra Raiders. Despite being a relatively inexperienced squad, the...

item.title: Over 800 Citizen Concerns Addressed via Health Ministry's 'Talk to the Minister' Platform
item.description: Dar es Salaam – More than 800 issues from citizens and health workers have been addressed through the digital 'Talk to the Minister' platform, the Ministry of Health has confirmed. Health Minister Moh...

item.title: Legal Protections for Pakistanis Detained in Bahrain Amid Iran Conflict
item.description: On March 10, Bahrain authorities arrested six people from Asian countries, including five Pakistanis, for allegedly filming and sharing videos about the Iran conflict, which has escalated into a Middl...

item.title: US and Israel Intensify Airstrikes as Iran Rejects Ceasefire
item.description: The United States and Israel have escalated their military campaign against Iran, carrying out what US Defence Secretary Pete Hegseth called the 'most intense day of strikes' yet. Tehran residents rep...

item.title: Nehal Hashmi appointed as Sindh Governor following PM recommendation
item.description: On Friday, Nehal Hashmi, a senior PML-N leader, officially took the oath as the new governor of Sindh. The ceremony was held in Karachi and conducted by Sindh High Court Chief Justice Zafar Ahmed Rajp...
